PL/I Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare Statem.prefix: PL/I Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare Statement
body: ***  Copy an interactive SQL Statement after     ***
PL/I Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare Statem.description: The DECLARE CURSOR statement names a cursor and specifies a select-statement. The select-statement defines a set of rows that, conceptually, make up the result table.  Note that Declare Cursor is not operational - and can be coded in WORKING-STORAGE or in the PROCEDURE DIVISION.

/** Note - the following statement will end the program  **/
PL/I Embedded SQL: Table Join Example.description:
Embedded DB2 Select/From/Where (table join) statement with fictitious Table & Column names. 
Note that the  "Join Condition" **typically** matches (compares) the Primary Key of one table with with the Foreign Key of another table.

PL/I Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select.prefix: PPL/I1 Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select IN) Example
body: *** These declarations are provided for convenience *
LIST ('**** E R R O R  M E S S A G E S ***');
/** Note - the following statement will end the program  **/
PL/I Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select.description:
Embedded DB2 Select/From/Where (table join) statement with fictitious Table & Column names. 
Note that the  "Subquery" **typically** selects compares the Primary Key of the table referenced in the outer select, with with the Foreign Key of  table referenced in the sub-select. However, note that there are lots of other processing scenarios and use cases. This example uses the IN operator - which roughly translates to "equal any" ... in other words, return a row through the Outer Select where the column value equals any value returned by the sub-select.

PL/I Embedded SQL: Update Where Current.prefix: PL/I Embedded SQL: Update Where Current of Cursor
body: ***  Copy an interactive SQL Statement between   ***
***  Note that FOR UPDATE OF <column> must       ***
***    be specified at the end of the Select     ***
***    for every column that is to be updated    ***
*** These declarations are provided for convenience *
*** This code must be executed before a Fetch statement ***
** It must be executed AFTER the Cursor Open statement ***
** and is typically executed in a Loop.                ***
** Copy select columns expression here, and prefix     ***
*** This code should be executed after you're finished ***
****  processing all of the cursor result rows         ***
LIST ('**** E R R O R  M E S S A G E S ***');
/** Note - the following statement will end the program  **/
PL/I Embedded SQL: Update Where Current.description: When your program has positioned the cursor on a row, you can update the row by using the UPDATE statement with the WHERE CURRENT OF clause. The WHERE CURRENT OF clause specifies a cursor that points to the row that you want to update.

PL/I Embedded SQL: Delete Where Current.prefix: PL/I Embedded SQL: Delete Where Current of Cursor
body: ***  Copy an interactive SQL Statement between   ***
***  Modify any host variable values with        ***
*** These declarations are provided for convenience *
*** This code must be placed in the Procedure Division ***
****  and must be executed before a Fetch statement    ***
** This code must be executed AFTER the Cursor Open    ***
** statement and is typically executed in a Loop.      ***
** Copy select columns expression here, and prefix     ***
*** This code should be executed after you're finished ***
***   processing all of the cursor result rows         ***
LIST ('**** E R R O R  M E S S A G E S ***');
/** Note - the following statement will end the program  **/
PL/I Embedded SQL: Delete Where Current.description: When your program has retrieved the current row, you can delete the row by using the DELETE statement with the WHERE CURRENT OF clause. The WHERE CURRENT OF clause specifies a cursor that points to the row that you want to delete.

PL/I EXEC CICS: WRITEQ TD.description: Command to Write data to a transient queue.

PL/I EXEC CICS: Date/Time Handling.description: Commands to Request system date/time and to transform into specific format.

PL/I EXEC CICS: DUMP TRANSACTION.description: Command to Request a transaction dump.

PL/I EXEC CICS: SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK.description: Command to back out changes and create a new unit of work.

PL/I EXEC CICS: ASSIGN NOHANDLE.description: Command to Get values from outside local environment.

PL/I EXEC CICS: RETRIEVE.description: Command to Retrieve data stored for a task.

PL/I EXEC CICS: REWRITE.description: Command to Update a record in a file.

PL/I EXEC CICS: READ UPDATE.description: Command to Read and Update a record in a CICS maintained data table.

PL/I EXEC CICS: DELETE.description: Command to Delete a record from a file.

PL/I EXEC CICS: READ.description: Command to Read a record from a file.

PL/I EXEC CICS: WRITE.description: Command to Write a record to a file.

PL/I EXEC CICS: ABEND.description: Command to Terminate a task abnormally.

PL/I EXEC CICS: XCTL.description: Command to not return control to calling program.

PL/I EXEC CICS: LINK.description: Command to Link to another program expecting return.

PL/I EXEC CICS: START.description: Command to Start a task at a specified time.

PL/I EXEC CICS: SEND CONTROL.description: Command to Send control to a terminal without map or text data.

PL/I EXEC CICS: SELECT EIBAID.description: Command to Evaluate the EIBAID field.
body: /* Processing is dependant upon the key that was pressed and
* field EIBAID contains this information.  EIBAID is set to
* dfhpf3 when function key 3 has been pressed, DFHCLEAR for
* the clear/pause key and DFHENTER for the enter key.
* key testing is necessary to determine the action required.

PL/I EXEC CICS: GETMAIN.description: Command to Allocate a main storage area.

PL/I EXEC CICS: HANDLE ABEND.description: Command to Handle an abnormal termination exit.

PL/I EXEC CICS: ASSIGN.description: Command to Request values from outside the local environment.

PL/I EXEC CICS: SEND MAP.description: Command to Send data to the terminal screen.

PL/I IMS: Complete PL/I IMS DB Program.prefix: PL/I IMS: Complete PL/I IMS DB Program
PL/I IMS: Complete PL/I IMS DB Program.description: Sample template of IMS DB Program
body: PLITDLI ENTRY,                           /* DL/I WIlL BE CALLED */
DFSOAST ENTRY OPTIONS (ASSEMBLER INTER), /* STATISTICS PRINT    */
DFSOAER ENTRY OPTIONS (ASSEMBLER INTER), /* STATUS COOE PRINT   */
02 DlPCKFBL FIXED BINARY(31),          /* KEY FEEOBACK LNG */
02 DlPCNSSG FIXED BINARY(3l),          /* N0. OF SENSEGS */
/* DECLARE FUNCTION COOES, I/0 AREA, CALL ARG LIST LENGTHS */
GU_FUNC STATIC CHAR (4) INIT t’GU’I,           /* CALL FUNCTION */
FOUR    STATIC FIXED BINARY (31) INIT I4 ),    /* ARG LIST LENGTH */
ERROPT1 CHAR (4) INIT (’0’) STATIC,            /* OPTN FOR DFSOAER */
ERROPT2 CHAR (4) INIT (’2’) STATIC,            /* FINAL OPTN:DFSOAER*/
DERRID  CHAR (8) INIT (’DERFORO1’) STATIC;     /* ID FOR DFSOAER */
/* DECLARE SEGMENT SEARCH AFGUMENT (SSA) - ORDER SEGMENT. */
IF DlPCSTAT -- ’ ’ THEN                      /* CALL EROOR PRINT */
CALL DFSOAER (DlPC,DERRID,IO AREA,ERROPT2); /* FINAL CALL TO ERR*/

body: DCL  01 SSA_NAME,             /* Fill in SSA fields below */
DCL  01 MSG_SEG_IO_AREA,      /* Fill in MSG IO area below */
DCL  01 DB_SEG_IO_AREA,       /* Fill in DB seq area below */
DCL  01 ALT_MSG_SEG_OUT,      /* Fill in ALT MSG area below */
DCL  01 IOPCB,                /* Fill in IO PCB area below */
DCL  01 ALTPCB,               /* Fill in ALT PCB area below */
DCL  01 DBPCB,                /* Fill in DB PCB area below */
